VISUAL: Framework de priorização para onboarding: o que ensinar primeiro para reps novos começarem a produzir rápido, timeline comprimida, foco em viabilidade imediata
[179:45] So, um, it's a little bit deviation from some of the stuff in the presentation, but generally speaking, when you bring on when you bring on sales guys for whatever offer you're looking to build a team and develop this type of stuff into them so they can close.
[180:10] Obviously, if you have an offer that's come on already, you have a batch, you have a compressed timeline, get people with them viability. what do you do or how like is your priority and how you approach just this information to them so they can get in and they can start getting fucking done and start putting number.
[180:35] So I heard you right. You're kind of talking about what's kind of like the immediate process that somebody can follow to kind of follow up once they're in a funnel or Yeah. Like there's a lot of interior input here and I guess it's like if we're going from like when to use what? Yeah. I'll get somebody from like zero to closing like what's it like what do I need to make sure that they implement now so we can like what what's what's highest priority for
[181:05] love it great question that is a great question good job uh so I have a process my team follows this process a lot of clients follow this process particularly with uh inbound opportunities that are like hot and ready.
[181:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Immediate Follow-Up Process for Hot Leads"
NÚMEROS: 5-10 minutos (primeiro contato), 1 hora (primeira call), 2 horas (se sem resposta), 2:00 PM quarta-feira (exemplo de agendamento)
VISUAL: Processo passo-a-passo para leads quentes: 1) Email de confirmação em 5-10min, 2) Selfie video via texto, 3) Call em 1 hora, 4) Sequências automáticas baseadas em outcome
[181:50] you're not talking about you're dark in my pipeline haven't called me back in three months like you said you would now I'm gonna implement something else this is immediate.
[182:05] So the immediately if somebody fills out a form, they're looking for information and it's high ticket. The very first thing that happens is they're going to get that confirmation email in the first 5 to 10 minutes afterwards of filling it out.
[182:30] I want that first touch to be immediate with if you want to if if for some reason it's 2 o'clock in the morning and we're all asleep, like I'm not going to hit you with a selfie video first. So I want somebody to be able to schedule something. I leave it up to them.
[182:50] But if you hit my calendar, you hit my CRM inbound, whatever, the first thing I'm going to do is I'm going to grab my cell phone. I'm going to type in your number or I'll look you up in my HubSpot app and then I just hit text message. Pulls up the text messages.
[183:15] The very first thing that you are getting from Jordan Stewart and my team if they're following my directions is they're getting a what's up Chuck Jordan over here from Stuart's office. Wanted to put a face to the name, let you know I'm a real person.
[183:40] Want to let you know I'm excited to meet with you at 2 o'clock on Wednesday. Like you filled that on the calendar. By the way, this is my personal sale. you got any questions, you need anything or you just want to get something done today, call me. Adios. Take it easy.
[184:05] That's uh touch number one and that is immediate. Uh because that is outside of our CRM. We do uh manually log that and like I'm fucking You don't you don't log that and it doesn't get done. Like dude, you're breaking my process, which means that you're going to break your sale more than likely.
[184:35] Then uh what they're going to do is they're going to subs uh subscribe you to a sequence based on your call outcome. So they're going to call you probably about an hour after sending that selfie video.
[184:55] We do know that most people do prefer to text. Text is the new phone call. So why not do that? If you don't text us back, you can't schedule an appointment or you don't get back with us within about an hour or two, that task to call you is going to pop up.
[185:20] based on that call, whether it's a voicemail, connected, scheduled appointment, whatever. Then a specific sequence is going to end up happening with emails, texts, and phone calls based on the timing.
[185:40] And if we and that's going to help do some of the heavy lifting of getting you scheduled, making sure we're emailing you case studies, testimonials, uh, social proof, blah blah blah to help solidify the deal.
[186:00] And then all we're looking for is we're looking for once something gets booked with this specific meeting name, shuts up everything, and then sends them a personal email from me.
[186:20] I'm like, "Hey, I saw you just booked with my guy Dale. His job is super simple. He's just going to provide you with all the information that you're looking for so that you can make the best decision moving forward. If you have any questions, email me personally. I run I run the shop over here. No red tape.
[186:45] Um, and so it's it's kind of a pretty quick cadence, but it's all very uh very bite-sized. There's not like a lengthy email or like a 10-page PDF that I send. It's all just very like very rapid pace kind of high value and then we just want to get to the meeting.
[187:10] We're not here to close you on shit. My job is simple. I just want to give you the information that you need so you can make the right decision. That increases show rates, gives people more confidence that they're working with a professional and kind of helps lower their guard.
[187:35] because obviously what are we going to do on that closing call? Close the fucking out. That's what we're going to do. But I don't want you to think that way when you're booking a call.
[187:55] So hopefully that gives you an idea of my process a bit of the uh scalable version of it and a little bit of that unscalable where you're getting that selfie video and you're getting an actual manually dialed phone call from my team to help personalize those first couple of uh hours that you're actually in the funnel. Does that help?
[188:20] Cool. Well then y'all, this is it. It's all I got for you. I'm going to be probably eating soon, jumping on a plane. So you want to say hi, say hi. If you want a photo, grab a photo. But I love you guys. I appreciate it. And thank you. All right, guys.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
$250,000 investido em ads com baixo ROI de fechamento
$62,500 - Contrato fechado com vídeo de follow-up (Glass Company)
$60,000 - Contrato fechado com vídeo de encanamento (Pride Plumbing)
$36,000 - Contrato fechado após 2.5 anos de follow-up diário (Murfreesboro Toyota)
$12,000 - Contrato fechado com vídeo Disney World (Managed IT Atlanta)
$5,000 - Comissão de deal após 2.5 anos de persistência
$250,000 - Investimento total em tecnologia (Dezembro)
$54,000 - Contrato anual ZoomInfo
$3 milhões/ano - Receita atual do negócio
$10 milhões - Meta anual
18-20 meses - Tempo para construir negócio multi-milhão
300% - Crescimento ano sobre ano
25,000+ - Total de contatos no CRM
21,000 - Registros ativos no CRM
10 pessoas - Equipe de vendas
300 calls/dia - Meta por vendedor
4 horas - Talk time mínimo diário
20-30 conexões/dia - Meta de conversas
50% primeiro mês + 15% recurring - Estrutura de comissão
75% entretenimento + 25% educação - Fórmula de follow-up
60% - Taxa de match do ZoomInfo
110 - Lead score alto (pronto para fechar)
-10 - Lead score negativo (desqualificado)
30 pontos - Threshold para distribuição de leads
90 dias - Período de avaliação de novos vendedores
2 minutos - Regra máxima de espera no Zoom
$2,500/mês - Custo do parceiro HubSpot (Evenbound)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Follow-Up:
Bang Triangle: 75% Entertainment + 25% Education = Relationship
Selfie videos customizados por indústria
Memes via ImgFlip.com (exemplo: esqueleto esperando)
Vídeos humorísticos específicos (Dale no sofá, banheiro com desentupidor, Disney World)
Follow-up de 2.5 anos com emails e voicemails diários únicos
Nunca repetir mesma mensagem
Sempre liderar com conteúdo de valor, não com "checking in"
Girl Scout analogy: persistência diária até venda
Tech Stack:
Shopify → HubSpot (integração nativa)
ZoomInfo para enriquecimento automático de dados
Kixie power dialer (4-10 linhas simultâneas)
Lead scoring dinâmico
Sequências automatizadas baseadas em comportamento
Workflow complexo com múltiplos pontos de decisão
Code tracking cross-domain
Sales Process:
Lead compra deck de cards → Shopify
Dados vão automaticamente para HubSpot
ZoomInfo enriquece contato (título, empresa, receita, funcionários, intent topics)
Marketing emails automatizados (drip sequence)
Lead score sobe baseado em engajamento
Ao atingir threshold (30 pontos) → Round robin para vendedor
Vendedor envia selfie video + email personalizado
Call manual após 1-2 horas
Sequências automatizadas continuam até booking
Após booking → automações param, vendedor assume
Scalable vs Non-Scalable:
Scalable: Mass emails, power dialing, automações, marketing workflows
Non-Scalable: Selfie videos, memes personalizados, research individual, calls manuais
Usar lead score para decidir quem merece atividades non-scalable
Priorizar leads 50-100+ para esforço manual
ESTRUTURA:
Four Quadrants Time Management:
Bottom Right (PRIORIDADE 1): Ruim nisso + Odeia (ex: impostos, planilhas, contabilidade)
Bottom Left (PRIORIDADE 2): Bom nisso + Odeia (ex: cold calling, fulfillment)
Top Right (PRIORIDADE 3): Bom nisso + Não se importa (ex: landing pages, edição vídeo)
Top Left (OBJETIVO 100%): Melhor que todos + Ama (ex: palestras, coaching, conteúdo, VSLs)
Lead Scoring System:
Positivo: Opens, clicks, page views, form fills, repeat purchases, ad clicks, call time
Negativo: Unsubscribe (-7,000), bounce (deletar), bad fit (remover)
Thresholds: 30+ (distribuir), 50-100+ (hot leads), 80+ (Magical Playground)
Sales Team Structure:
Setters (primeiros 90 dias)
Closers (após 90 dias + aprovação)
Todos fazem cold calling quando não em demos
Métricas rígidas: 300 calls OU 4h talk time diário
Accountability extremo (história de quarta-feira 3PM-6PM)
Commission Structure:
Outbound/Full Cycle: 50% primeiro mês + 15% recurring vitalício
Inbound Only: 100% primeiro mês
Bônus por volume mensal/trimestral
Incentivo para nurturing de longo prazo
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Investimentos:
$250,000 - Overhaul completo de tecnologia (Dezembro)
$54,000 - ZoomInfo (anual)
$2,500/mês - Evenbound (HubSpot partner)
$20,000-30,000 - Business coaching (Dan Sullivan)
Deals Fechados:
$62,500 - Glass Designs (vídeo Dale sofá)
$60,000 - Pride Plumbing LA (vídeo banheiro)
$36,000 - Murfreesboro Toyota (2.5 anos follow-up)
$12,000 - Managed IT Atlanta (vídeo Disney)
$25,000/mês - Deal exemplo para comissão ($12,500 upfront)
Business Performance:
$3M/ano - Receita atual
$10M/ano - Meta próximo ano
300% - Crescimento YoY
18-20 meses - Tempo para construir de zero a multi-milhão
10% - Taxa de conversão estimada de lead pool
Time Value Examples:
$250,000/ano ÷ 12 ÷ 30 ÷ 24 = $14.82/hora
3h/semana lavanderia × $1,000/hora = $12,000/mês desperdiçado
10h/mês recuperadas × $3,500/hora = $35,000 valor vs. $10,000 investimento
Pricing Philosophy:
$1,200/mês - Exemplo de underpricing (cliente de marketing)
$3,000 upfront + $1,200/mês - Estrutura inadequada
$30,000/mês × 12 meses - Contrato B2B ideal vs. $299/mês individual
$5,000/mês - Serviço premium com monetização vs. $500-1,000/mês básico
FILOSOFIAS-CHAVE:
"Always Bring Value" vs. "Always Be Closing"
"Who Not How" - Contratar expertise ao invés de aprender
"Sell The Way You Buy" - Suas objeções criam objeções de clientes
"Want the Deal, Don't Need the Deal" - Eliminar desespero
"5% Growth = 5% Decline" - Rejeitar crescimento incremental
"Push Up or Push Out" - Pipeline agressivo
"Rich People Don't Wait" - 2 minutos máximo
"Dating 100 People" - Pipeline robusto elimina medo
"Stop Adding, Start Subtracting" - Crescer por eliminação
DURAÇÃO: 188 minutos (3h 8min) FORMATO: Apresentação ao vivo com Q&A, Inner Circle Event
